title: Tutorial 8 — Analysis Settings, Loads, and Supports
problem: Starting from the provided Globe_Valve_SS07_Start.wbpz archive, select all 55 internal 'wetted' surfaces of the globe valve (48 on ValveBody, 2 on flange, 5 on seal) and apply a 7 MPa Pressure load. Apply Frictionless Supports to both end-flange faces and to all 9 bolt-hole surfaces on each end flange. Solve, then review Total Deformation and Equivalent (von-Mises) Stress.

_notes.source: Built from ANSYS Mechanical Getting Started, Release 2023 R1 — Module 07 Student Step-by-Step Guide: Analysis Settings, Loads, and Supports.
_notes.selector_status: UNVERIFIED — selectors use display names/labels taken from the source slide screenshots, following the same convention as tut1.json. Resolve real UIA automation_id/name via live testing before relying on them.
_notes.highlight_legend: uia = overlay draws a box around a UIA element. ocr_text = overlay finds on-screen text via OCR (used for the Workbench Toolbox / Project Schematic, which are not exposed to UIA — see tut1.json's workbench_uia_finding note). instruction = 3D-viewport action UIA cannot see; shows a reference screenshot instead of a tracked box. none = no highlight (launch).
_notes.verify_legend: Solve and the two 'Insert > result' actions use verify.type == "script"/"uia" (real, checkable model-state changes), matching how tut1.json verifies its solve/result steps. All face-selection and review steps use verify.type == "manual", per tut1's manual_verify_switch_2026-06 decision for anything not worth chasing OCR/UIA fragility on for a student-facing pilot.
_notes.report_checks_rationale: Unlike tut5 (Connections) and tut6 (Mesh), this module ends in an actual solved result with a clearly-labeled numeric answer in the source slide (Equivalent Stress Max: 133.15 MPa), so report_checks.expected_result is populated the same way tut1.json's is — tolerance is a rough placeholder (±5 MPa) since the exact converged value depends on the mesh settings carried over from Module 06 and should be confirmed by re-running the tutorial live.
_notes.wetted_surfaces_step: me_02_select_wetted_surfaces stands in for the full 55-face selection process (48 ValveBody + 2 flange + 5 seal) called out on slide 4; the slide explicitly says to use a Section Plane (me_01) and the same technique as Module 01's Step-by-Step Guide, so this tutorial doesn't re-derive that technique — the hint just points back to it.
_notes.wb_steps_reused: wb_01_open / wb_02_open_archive / wb_03_edit_model mirror the same three steps in tut5_connections.json and tut6_mesh.json (only the archive filename differs: Globe_Valve_SS07_Start.wbpz here), since every module in this series starts the same way — open Workbench, open the starter archive, edit the Model cell into Mechanical.

sections.section: Open the starter project in Workbench

steps.title: Open Ansys Workbench
steps.description: Launch Ansys Workbench: Windows Start Menu button → All apps → ANSYS nn.n → Workbench nn.n.
hints: Start it from the Windows Start menu or the lab launcher.

steps.title: Open the starter archive
steps.description: File → Open…, browse for archive file Globe_Valve_SS07_Start.wbpz, click Open, then save it to a convenient location.

verify.prompt: Project opened and saved to a convenient location?

hints: Click File to open the menu, then choose Open…

steps.title: Edit the Model cell
steps.description: Right-click the Model cell in the Static Structural system block and choose Edit… to launch Ansys Mechanical.
hints: The system block is in the Project Schematic; 'Model' is the row with the lightning-bolt icon.

sections.section: Select the wetted surfaces for the pressure load

steps.title: Define Section Plane 1
steps.description: Define Section Plane 1 to cut through the assembly, for ease in viewing the internal 'wetted' surfaces.

verify.prompt: Section Plane 1 created, exposing the interior of the valve, flange, and seal?

hints: The Section Plane tool is on the Graphics toolbar above the Geometry view.

steps.title: Select all 55 wetted surfaces
steps.description: Ctrl+click to select all internal 'wetted' surfaces: 48 on the ValveBody part, 2 on the flange part, and 5 on the seal part, for a total of 55 surfaces.

verify.prompt: 55 faces selected in total (48 ValveBody + 2 flange + 5 seal)?

hints: Use the same technique as the Module 01 Step-by-Step Guide for the surfaces on the valve body.
Rotate the model with the section plane active to reach hidden faces.

steps.title: Insert a Pressure load
steps.description: Right-click Static Structural in the Outline tree → Insert → Pressure, scoped to the 55 faces just selected.
hints: Right-click directly on 'Static Structural', not on an individual body.

steps.title: Set the Pressure magnitude to 7 MPa
steps.description: In the Pressure Details, set Magnitude to 7 MPa.
hints: Magnitude is in the Definition section of Details.

sections.section: Apply Frictionless Supports to the end flanges

steps.title: Select one end flange face
steps.description: Click one end flange surface in the Geometry view.
hints: This is the flat annular face at the very end of one branch of the valve.

steps.title: Insert Frictionless Support on it
steps.description: Right-click Static Structural → Insert → Frictionless Support, scoped to the selected flange face.
hints: This becomes 'Frictionless Support' in the Outline tree.

steps.title: Select the other end flange face
steps.description: Click the other end flange surface in the Geometry view.

verify.prompt: The opposite end flange face highlighted?

hints: This is the flat annular face at the end of the other branch.

steps.title: Insert Frictionless Support on it
steps.description: Right-click Static Structural → Insert → Frictionless Support, scoped to this second flange face.
hints: This becomes 'Frictionless Support 2' in the Outline tree.

sections.section: Apply Frictionless Supports to the bolt holes

steps.title: Select the 9 bolt-hole surfaces on one end flange
steps.description: Ctrl+click to select all 9 cylindrical hole surfaces on one end flange.

verify.prompt: All 9 cylindrical hole faces on this flange selected?

hints: Rotate the view to make sure all 9 holes around the bolt circle are included.

steps.title: Insert Frictionless Support on the holes
steps.description: Right-click Static Structural → Insert → Frictionless Support, scoped to the 9 selected hole faces.
hints: This becomes 'Frictionless Support 3' in the Outline tree.

steps.title: Select the 9 bolt-hole surfaces on the other end flange
steps.description: Ctrl+click to select all 9 cylindrical hole surfaces on the other end flange.

verify.prompt: All 9 cylindrical hole faces on the second flange selected?

hints: Same technique as the previous flange, on the opposite end.

steps.title: Insert Frictionless Support on the holes
steps.description: Right-click Static Structural → Insert → Frictionless Support, scoped to these 9 selected hole faces.
hints: This becomes 'Frictionless Support 4' in the Outline tree.

steps.title: Review the loads and supports graphically
steps.description: Select the environment (Static Structural) branch and review the graphics display: Pressure (A), and Frictionless Supports (B, C, D, E) should all be visible with their annotation labels.

verify.prompt: All 5 labeled loads/supports (A-E) visible in the Geometry view?

hints: Pressure is usually labeled A (red); the four Frictionless Supports are B-E (blue/purple).

steps.title: Solve
steps.description: Click Solve on the Home ribbon.
hints: Solving may take a moment; watch the status bar.

steps.title: Insert Total Deformation
steps.description: Right-click Solution → Insert → Deformation → Total.
hints: Solution is under the Static Structural branch in the Outline.

steps.title: Insert Equivalent Stress
steps.description: Right-click Solution → Insert → Stress → Equivalent.
hints: Same right-click menu, this time choose Stress > Equivalent.

steps.title: Evaluate all results
steps.description: Right-click Solution and choose Evaluate All Results.
hints: Both Total Deformation and Equivalent Stress evaluate together.

steps.title: Review Total Deformation
steps.description: Select the Total Deformation branch to review the deformation contour result.

verify.prompt: Total Deformation contour reviewed in the Geometry view?

hints: The legend on the left shows the Min/Max deformation values.

steps.title: Review Equivalent Stress
steps.description: Select the Equivalent Stress branch to review the von-Mises stress contour result. Expected maximum is approximately 133.15 MPa.

verify.prompt: Equivalent Stress contour reviewed, with a Max around 133.15 MPa?

hints: The highest stress concentrations appear near the bolt holes and the body-to-bonnet junction.
